---
title: "R Notebook"
output: html_notebook
---

This is an [R Markdown](http://rmarkdown.rstudio.com) Notebook. When you execute code within the notebook, the results appear beneath the code. 

Try executing this chunk by clicking the *Run* button within the chunk or by placing your cursor inside it and pressing *Cmd+Shift+Enter*. 

```{r}
# gets utility functions
source('utility_functions.R')
#import data from bundles
bundle <- readRDS("filtered_data_bundle_updated.rds")
filtered_data_24 <- bundle$data
rate_variables_24 <- bundle$rate_vars

bundle23 <- readRDS('filtered_data_bundle_23.rds')
rate_variables_23 <- bundle23$rate_vars
```
```{r}
#PCA plots
filtered_data_23 <- filtered_data_23 |>
  filter(season == 2023 & player_id != '' & games_played > 10 & team_club_name != '' & player_position != 'Goalkeeper')

data_both_seasons <- bind_rows(filtered_data_23, filtered_data_24)

# elbow plot --------------------------------------------------------------
elbow_both <- prcomp(dplyr::select(data_both_seasons, all_of(rate_variables_24)),
                   center = TRUE, scale. = TRUE)
fviz_eig(elbow_both, addlabels = TRUE) + 
  geom_hline(yintercept = 100 * (1 / ncol(elbow_both$x)), linetype = "dashed", color = "darkred")

# actual graphs -----------------------------------------------------------
#2024
by_position_general_pca_24 <- plot_pca(filtered_data_24, rate_variables_24,
                                    "player_position") + 
  geom_text_repel(aes(label = player_match_name), size = 3, max.overlaps = 10)
by_variable_team_pca_24 <- plot_pca(filtered_data_24, rate_variables_24,
                                    "player_position") + scale_shape_manual(values = 0:13) + facet_wrap(~team_abbreviation)
by_cluster_team_pca_24 <- plot_pca(filtered_data_24, rate_variables_24,
                                   "cluster") + facet_wrap(~team_abbreviation)
by_position_general_pca_24
by_variable_team_pca_24
# by_cluster_team_pca_24

#2023
by_position_general_pca_23 <- plot_pca(filtered_data_23, rate_variables_23,
                                       "player_position") +
  geom_text_repel(aes(label = player_match_name), size = 3, max.overlaps = 10)
by_position_team_pca_23 <- plot_pca(filtered_data_23, rate_variables_23,
                                       "player_position")
by_position_general_pca_23 
by_position_team_pca_23 + facet_wrap(~team_abbreviation)


```
```{r}
# dendrograms

plot_dendrogram_position_leaves_cluster_branches <- function(data_matrix, metadata, dist_method = "euclidean", linkage = "complete", k_clusters = 7) {
  # Load required package
  if (!requireNamespace("dendextend", quietly = TRUE)) {
    install.packages("dendextend")
  }
  library(dendextend)
  
  # Step 1: Compute distance and clustering
  dist_matrix <- dist(data_matrix, method = dist_method)
  hc <- hclust(dist_matrix, method = linkage)
  dend <- as.dendrogram(hc)
  
  # Step 2: Match labels to player names
  labels(dend) <- metadata$player_match_name[match(labels(dend), rownames(data_matrix))]
  
  # Step 3: Cut tree into clusters
  clusters <- cutree(hc, k = k_clusters)
  metadata$cluster <- factor(clusters)
  
  # Step 4: Define position colors
  position_colors <- c(
    "Attacker" = "red",
    "Midfielder" = "blue",
    "Defender" = "green"
  )
  
  # Step 5: Color branches by cluster
  dend <- color_branches(dend, k = k_clusters)
  
  # Step 6: Color leaf nodes by position
  colLab <- function(n) {
    if (is.leaf(n)) {
      a <- attributes(n)
      label <- a$label
      i <- which(metadata$player_match_name == label)
      
      if (length(i) == 0) {
        color <- "gray"
      } else {
        pos <- metadata$player_position[i][1]  # Safely take first match
        color <- position_colors[[pos]]
        if (is.null(color)) color <- "gray"
      }
      
      attr(n, "nodePar") <- c(
        a$nodePar,
        list(
          lab.col = NA,     # No label color
          col = color,      # point color by position
          pch = 20,
          lab.cex = 0,      # Hide label text
          cex = 0.5
        )
      )
    }
    return(n)
  }
  
  # Step 7: Apply leaf coloring
  dend <- dendrapply(dend, colLab)
  
  # Step 8: Plot without labels
  plot(dend,
       ylab = "Pairwise Distance", 
       xlab = "Players", leaflab = "none")
  
  # Step 9: Legends
  legend("topright", legend = paste("Cluster", 1:k_clusters),
         col = rainbow(k_clusters), lwd = 2, cex = 0.9, bty = "n",
         inset = c(0, -0.03))
  
  legend("topleft", legend = names(position_colors),
         col = position_colors, pch = 20, pt.cex = 1.5, cex = 0.9, bty = "n",
         inset = c(0.3, 0))
}


plot_dendrogram_position_leaves_cluster_branches(nwsl_quant_24, filtered_data_24, k_clusters = 7)
plot_dendrogram_position_leaves_cluster_branches(nwsl_quant_23, filtered_data_23, k_clusters = 7)
```
```{r}

# chi-squared-test --------------------------------------------------------

cluster_team_frequency_table <- function(df){
  df |>
    count(team_abbreviation, cluster) |>
    pivot_wider(names_from = cluster, values_from = n, values_fill = 0)
}

frequency_table_24 <- cluster_team_frequency_table(players_in_each_cluster)
frequency_table_23 <- cluster_team_frequency_table(cluster_team_summary_23)
chi_squared_test <- function(freq_table){
  chisq_matrix <- as.matrix(freq_table[,-1])
  chisq.test(chisq_matrix)
}

print(chi_squared_test(frequency_table_24))

print(chi_squared_test(frequency_table_23))
```
```{r}
# cluster summary  ---------------------------------------------------------------
important_variables <- c('player_match_name', 'cluster', 'player_position', 'team_abbreviation')

get_players_in_each_cluster <- function(df, variables_of_intrests_list, focus_variable){
 df |>
    select(all_of(variables_of_intrests_list)) |>
    arrange({{focus_variable}}) 
}

get_cluster_summary <- function(df, variables_of_intrests_list, focus_variable){
  players_in_each_cluster <- get_players_in_each_cluster(df, variables_of_intrests_list, focus_variable)
  cluster_summary <- players_in_each_cluster |>
    group_by(cluster) |>
    summarize(
      total_players_in_cluster = n(),
      attackers = sum(player_position == 'Attacker'),
      midfielders = sum(player_position == "Midfielder"),
      defenders = sum(player_position == 'Defender'),
      attacker_rate = attackers / total_players_in_cluster,
      midfielder_rate = midfielders / total_players_in_cluster,
      defender_rate = defenders / total_players_in_cluster
    )
  cluster_summary <- as.data.frame(cluster_summary)
  
  return(cluster_summary)
}

get_team_cluster_summary <- function(df, variables_of_intrests_list, focus_variable) {
  team_cluster_breakdown <- df |>
    group_by(cluster, team_abbreviation) |>
    summarize(
      players_in_team_cluster = n(),
      .groups = "drop") 

  return(as.data.frame(team_cluster_breakdown))
}

# call functions here -----------------------------------------------------
cluster_summary_24 <- get_cluster_summary(filtered_data_24, important_variables, 'cluster')
cluster_centriods_24 <- get_cluster_centriods(filtered_data_24, rate_variables, cluster)
cluster_summary_23 <- get_cluster_summary(filtered_data_23, important_variables, 'cluster')
cluster_centriods_23 <- get_cluster_centriods(filtered_data_23, rate_variables, cluster)
cluster_team_summary_23 <- get_team_cluster_summary(filtered_data_23, rate_variables, 'cluster')

# cluster centriods -------------------------------------------------------

get_cluster_centriods <- function(df, interest_variables, focus_variable){
  cluster_overview <- df |>
    group_by({{focus_variable}}) |>
    summarise(across(all_of(interest_variables), list(mean = mean, sd = sd), .names = "{.col}_{.fn}")) 
  cluster_centriods <- df |>
    group_by({{focus_variable}}) |>
    summarize(across(all_of(interest_variables), mean))
}
```
```{r}

# imports -----------------------------------------------------------------
source('utility_functions.R')
bundle <- readRDS("filtered_data_bundle.rds")
rate_variables <- bundle$rate_vars

selected_variables <- c(
  "rate_touches",
  "rate_successful_passes_total",
  "rate_short_passes_successful",
  "rate_loss_of_possession_total",
  "rate_duels_ground",
  "rate_passes_forward",
  "rate_duels_lost",
  "rate_duels_ground_lost",
  "rate_short_passes_unsuccessful",
  "rate_passes_unsuccessful"
)

# standardization ---------------------------------------------------------
get_mean_stdev <- function(df, variables) {
  results <- list()
  
  for (var in variables) {
    x <- df[[var]]
    mean_x <- mean(x)
    stdev_x <- sd(x)
    
    results[[var]] <- list(mean = mean_x, stdev = stdev_x)
  }
  return(results)
}

standard_vars_24 <- get_mean_stdev(filtered_data_24, rate_variables)

# compare to centriod -----------------------------------------------------
standardize_centriods <- function(centriods_df, standardized_variables, variables){
  results <- (centriods_df)
  for (var in variables){
    mean <- standardized_variables[[var]]$mean
    stdev <- standardized_variables[[var]]$stdev
    x <- centriods_df[[var]]
    results[[var]] <- (x - mean) /stdev
  }
  return(results)
}

std_centriods_24 <- standardize_centriods(cluster_centriods_24, standard_vars_24, rate_variables)
std_centriods_24$cluster <- as.factor(std_centriods_24$cluster)

std_centriods_23 <- standardize_centriods(cluster_centriods_23, standard_vars_24, rate_variables)
std_centriods_23$cluster <- as.factor(std_centriods_23$cluster)

# get most frequent position group ----------------------------------------
get_most_freq_position <- function(summary_df, analysis_df){
  summary_df<- summary_df |>
    rowwise() |> 
    mutate (
      
      primary_position_group = c("Attacker", "Defender", "Midfielder")[
        which.max(c(attacker_rate, defender_rate, midfielder_rate))
      ]
    ) |> 
    ungroup()
  analysis_df <- analysis_df |>
    left_join(
      summary_df |> select(cluster, primary_position_group),
      by = "cluster"
    )
}

positional_std_centriods_24 <- get_most_freq_position(cluster_summary_24, std_centriods_24)
positional_std_centriods_23 <- get_most_freq_position(cluster_summary_23, std_centriods_23)


# plot paracoord by position ----------------------------------------------
ggparcoord(positional_std_centriods_24, columns = all_of(selected_variables), 
           groupColumn = 'primary_position_group',
           order = match(selected_variables, names(positional_std_centriods_24))) + 
  labs(color = "Primary\nPosition\nGroup") +  
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 70, hjust = 1),
        legend.text = element_text(size = 8), 
        legend.title = element_text(size = 9),    
        legend.key.size = unit(0.5, "lines"))

ggparcoord(positional_std_centriods_23, columns = all_of(selected_variables), 
           groupColumn = 'primary_position_group',
           order = match(selected_variables, names(positional_std_centriods_23))) + 
  labs(color = "Primary\nPosition\nGroup") +  
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 70, hjust = 1),
        legend.text = element_text(size = 8), 
        legend.title = element_text(size = 9),    
        legend.key.size = unit(0.5, "lines")) 
```
```{r}
data_24 <- filtered_data_24 
data_23 <- filtered_data_23

common_vars <- intersect(names(data_23), names(data_24))

data_23 <- data_23[, common_vars]
data_24 <- data_24[, common_vars]


combine_data_get_rand <- function(prev_season, current_season, join_variable){
  common_names <- intersect(prev_season[[join_variable]], current_season[[join_variable]])
  print(length(common_names))
  df1 <- prev_season |> 
    filter(!!sym(join_variable) %in% common_names) |> 
    arrange(!!sym(join_variable)) |>
    distinct(player_match_name, .keep_all = TRUE)
  df2 <- current_season |> 
    filter(!!sym(join_variable) %in% common_names) |> 
    arrange(!!sym(join_variable)) |>
    distinct(player_match_name, .keep_all = TRUE)
  
  # 
  # print(nrow(df1))
  # print(nrow(df2))
  # print(identical(df1[[join_variable]], df2[[join_variable]]))
  # print(which(df1[[join_variable]] != df2[[join_variable]]))
  x <- table(df1$cluster, df2$cluster)
  rand_num <- rand_index(df1, df2, join_variable)
  
  return(rand_num)
}


rand_index <- function(df1, df2, join_variable){
  merged <- inner_join(
    df1[, c(join_variable, "cluster")],
    df2[, c(join_variable, "cluster")],
    by = join_variable,
    suffix = c("_1", "_2")
  )
  clusters1 <- merged$cluster_1
  clusters2 <- merged$cluster_2
  
  if (length(clusters1) != length(clusters2)) {
    stop("Cluster vectors are not the same length after join.")
  }
  
  print(head(merged))
  
  print(any(is.na(merged$cluster_1)))
  print(any(is.na(merged$cluster_2)))
  
  ari <- adjustedRandIndex(clusters1, clusters2)
  return(ari)
  
}

rand_23_24 <- combine_data_get_rand(data_23, data_24, "player_match_name")

rand_23_24
```

Add a new chunk by clicking the *Insert Chunk* button on the toolbar or by pressing *Cmd+Option+I*.

When you save the notebook, an HTML file containing the code and output will be saved alongside it (click the *Preview* button or press *Cmd+Shift+K* to preview the HTML file). 

The preview shows you a rendered HTML copy of the contents of the editor. Consequently, unlike *Knit*, *Preview* does not run any R code chunks. Instead, the output of the chunk when it was last run in the editor is displayed.
